questions.question: How much of the year does the prohibition of gas leaf blowers last? What date in the year does it start, and what date does it end?
questions.answer: Not specified in the provided sources.
questions.gap: The provided sources contain no information about gas leaf blower prohibitions, seasonal dates, or duration of any such prohibition. Best practice suggests a year-round prohibition with exceptions for peak leaf-falling seasons (e.g., March 15–May 15 and October 1–December 31), but this statute does not address gas leaf blowers at all.

Best practice recommends weekday hours of 8:00 a.m.–8:00 p.m., Saturday hours of 9:00 a.m.–8:00 p.m., Sunday/holiday hours of 9:00 a.m.–5:00 p.m., and a prohibition during June 1–August 31.
